shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files -g '"'!*node_modules*'"' -g '"'!*.lock'"' | head -200 && printf '\\n--- package manifests ---\\n' && rg --files -g 'package.json' -g 'next.config.*' -g 'app/**' -g 'src/**' | head -200"
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,220p' package.json && printf '\\n--- next config ---\\n' && sed -n '1,160p' next.config.ts && printf '\\n--- tsconfig ---\\n' && sed -n '1,180p' tsconfig.json"
/bin/bash -lc 'git diff -- app/page.tsx app/actions.ts lib/posts.ts && git status --short'
/bin/bash -lc 'nl -ba app/page.tsx | head -20 && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build'
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba app/actions.ts && printf '\\n--- posts ---\\n' && nl -ba lib/posts.ts && printf '\\n--- page ---\\n' && nl -ba app/page.tsx"
/bin/bash -lc 'nmem --json m search "Next.js updateTag posts cache invalidation Server Action stale"'

/bin/bash -lc "nmem --json m add \"For the posts Server Action, use Next.js updateTag('posts') after creating the record. The posts list is read through unstable_cache with tags: ['posts']. updateTag is the correct choice when the next render must block for fresh data and never serve stale cached content; revalidateTag('posts', 'max') permits stale-while-revalidate and does not meet that requirement.\" -t \"Posts creation requires immediate cache expiration\" --unit-type decision -l \"nextjs\" -l \"caching\" -s codex -i 0.9"
